default_binding.A1_LANDMARK2: a little card fallen behind it
default_binding.C1_PLAN: a folded plan with one shelf circled
default_binding.ROUTE_C_CACHE: a shallow gap behind the books
default_binding.RA_GLOBE: a great clockwork globe of brass rings
default_binding.RB_WORKSHOP: a dusty room crowded with half-built clockwork
default_binding.VB_GALLERY: a small sealed gallery of paintings

slots.meaning: The child protagonist.
slots.guidance: A single kid-relatable explorer; capable, curious, not reckless.

slots.meaning: The whole bounded, explorable place the story happens inside.
slots.guidance: A locked-for-the-night building the hero is briefly stuck inside; wonder over dread; no toxic air, no lethal hazard anywhere in it.

slots.meaning: The central landmark the hero starts near and that every later stage's paths reconverge on.
slots.guidance: A grand, safe, recurring architectural space; never a source of danger.

slots.meaning: The offstage historical figure whose hidden legacy drives the whole mystery.
slots.guidance: A benevolent, long-dead or long-retired figure; referenced only through objects and writing, never appears as a threat.

slots.meaning: The one adult figure who might catch the hero; the story's only human risk.
slots.guidance: Stern but fundamentally kind; any 'catch' is a safe, mildly embarrassing setback, never harm or real trouble.

slots.meaning: The trusted adult authority the hero can choose to bring the discovery to.
slots.guidance: Delighted and grateful, not angry; a warm authority figure.

slots.meaning: The recurring identifying mark that ties every clue and lock together.
slots.guidance: A small, consistent visual/tactile motif (a stamp, carving, or seal); purely a clue device, never dangerous to touch.

slots.meaning: The single object found at the first bottleneck that unlocks all three second-stage doors.
slots.guidance: A plain, safe-to-handle found object.

slots.meaning: The written cipher clue that must be paired with the cipher tool.
slots.guidance: A short paper or card clue; purely informational.

slots.meaning: The decoding device that, paired with the coded note, resolves into a direction.
slots.guidance: A harmless mechanical or paper decoding aid.

slots.meaning: The hero's own half-torn map, referenced whenever a clue lines up with it.
slots.guidance: A carried paper prop; purely informational, never a hazard.

slots.meaning: The overarching prize: what the founder hid and the whole mystery is ultimately about.
slots.guidance: A benign donated collection/treasure; a discovery to be shared, never a source of danger.

slots.meaning: The recurring landmark all three second-stage sub-routes converge on to read the cipher.
slots.guidance: A quiet, safe reading spot; a lamp-lit table.

slots.meaning: The named wing/gallery the hero can choose first (route A).
slots.guidance: A distinct, safe museum wing or room; distinguishable from the other two starting choices.

slots.meaning: The named wing/gallery the hero can choose first (route B).
slots.guidance: A distinct, safe museum wing or room; distinguishable from the other two starting choices.

slots.meaning: The named wing/gallery the hero can choose first (route C).
slots.guidance: A distinct, safe museum wing or room; distinguishable from the other two starting choices.

slots.meaning: Fork cue for A1 (the case track).
slots.guidance: A benign visual cue that draws attention to the display case.

slots.meaning: The first disturbed item found at the case.
slots.guidance: A safe, examinable disturbed display piece.

slots.meaning: The second disturbed item found at the case.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable fallen note/label.

slots.meaning: Fork cue for A2 (the alarm track).
slots.guidance: A benign, non-threatening sound cue.

slots.meaning: Ending: the freeze-and-get-caught setback on the alarm track.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal, non-toxic, unarmed; a kindly, embarrassing catch-and-escort, never real trouble. Must read as safe.

slots.meaning: The corridor both A sub-tracks converge on before the key bottleneck.
slots.guidance: A plain, safe back-of-house corridor.

slots.meaning: The locked obstacle at the end of route A's corridor.
slots.guidance: A door with no key yet in sight; not dangerous, just locked.

slots.meaning: Where the hidden key turns out to be, on route A.
slots.guidance: A safe, easily-pried hiding spot.

slots.meaning: Fork cue for B1 (the sarcophagus track).
slots.guidance: A benign historical display piece.

slots.meaning: The sarcophagus's loose lid, the B1a sub-choice.
slots.guidance: Safe and liftable; heavier-looking than it is.

slots.meaning: The B1b sub-choice: a nearby display with a missing piece.
slots.guidance: A safe display case with an empty mount.

slots.meaning: Fork cue for B2 (the map track).
slots.guidance: A benign historical document on the wall.

slots.meaning: The safe tool the hero can take on the B2 map track.
slots.guidance: A working, harmless hand torch.

slots.meaning: Ending: the roped-off gallery that traps the hero for the night on the B2 map track.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal, non-toxic, unarmed; the door locks her in safely until morning, not in danger. Must read as safe.

slots.meaning: The hall both B sub-tracks converge on before the key bottleneck.
slots.guidance: A plain, safe connecting hall.

slots.meaning: The locked obstacle at the end of route B's hall.
slots.guidance: A door with no key yet in sight; not dangerous, just locked.

slots.meaning: Where the hidden key turns out to be, on route B.
slots.guidance: A safe, easily-lifted hiding spot.

slots.meaning: Fork cue for C1 (the ledger track).
slots.guidance: A benign historical record book.

slots.meaning: The C1a sub-choice: the archive's card catalogue.
slots.guidance: A safe, browsable index.

slots.meaning: The C1b sub-choice: a stiff drawer in the same desk.
slots.guidance: A safe, if stubborn, drawer.

slots.meaning: The clue card found in the card index.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable single card.

slots.meaning: The clue found in the sticking drawer.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable floor plan.

slots.meaning: The locked receptacle deep in the stacks that the key opens.
slots.guidance: A plain locked cabinet; not dangerous, just locked.

slots.meaning: Where the hidden key turns out to be, on route C.
slots.guidance: A safe, hand-sized hiding spot.

slots.meaning: Fork cue for C2 (the dark-stacks track).
slots.guidance: A dim but harmless part of the archive.

slots.meaning: Ending: getting turned around blind in the dark stacks on the C2 track.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal, non-toxic, unarmed; sitting tight until morning, dusty but perfectly safe. Must read as safe.

slots.meaning: The safe light source the hero can find in the dark stacks.
slots.guidance: A working, harmless hand lantern.

slots.meaning: The first of the three doors the found key can open.
slots.guidance: The founder's old study; safe, dusty, undisturbed for decades.

slots.meaning: The second of the three doors/receptacles the found key can open.
slots.guidance: A sealed display case; safe, just stuck with age.

slots.meaning: The third of the three doors the found key can open.
slots.guidance: A plain back stairwell; safe to climb.

slots.meaning: The furniture searched in the founder's study.
slots.guidance: A safe, browsable antique desk.

slots.meaning: The portrait hiding a safe behind it.
slots.guidance: A safe, ordinary painting; nothing eerie or threatening.

slots.meaning: The clue-bearing item found in the desk.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable notebook.

slots.meaning: What the sealed display cabinet holds.
slots.guidance: Safe historical documents under glass.

slots.meaning: The clue-bearing item found in the display cabinet.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable ledger.

slots.meaning: Ending: forcing the display cabinet's latch too hard on the KEY_B track.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal, non-toxic, unarmed; only the display glass is harmed, the hero is unhurt, just embarrassed. Must read as safe.

slots.meaning: The furniture searched at the base of the service stairs.
slots.guidance: A safe, low cupboard.

slots.meaning: The clue-bearing item found in the cupboard.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable rolled chart.

slots.meaning: The short phrase the first cipher line resolves into.
slots.guidance: A safe, evocative instruction phrase.

slots.meaning: The first of the three named rooms the cipher's direction points to.
slots.guidance: A distinct, safe museum room; distinguishable from the other two cipher choices.

slots.meaning: The second of the three named rooms the cipher's direction points to.
slots.guidance: A distinct, safe museum room; distinguishable from the other two cipher choices.

slots.meaning: The third of the three named rooms the cipher's direction points to.
slots.guidance: A distinct, safe museum room; distinguishable from the other two cipher choices.

slots.meaning: The wonder-feature of the Astronomy Hall.
slots.guidance: A safe, awe-inducing painted ceiling.

slots.meaning: The shortcut option in the Astronomy Hall.
slots.guidance: A safe, sturdy ladder.

slots.meaning: The safe crossing high over the hall.
slots.guidance: A safe, if narrow, walkway with no fall risk when crossed with care.

slots.meaning: Ending: climbing too high into the dark dome instead of crossing the catwalk.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal, non-toxic, unarmed; a tired, dizzy climb back down, never a fall or real danger. Must read as safe.

slots.meaning: The interactive fixture in the Map Room.
slots.guidance: A safe, turnable floor globe.

slots.meaning: The alternative clue-bearing fixture in the Map Room.
slots.guidance: A safe, browsable oversized atlas.

slots.meaning: The room behind the Clock Corridor's ticking clocks.
slots.guidance: A safe mechanical room.

slots.meaning: The timed obstacle across the Clock Corridor's only doorway.
slots.guidance: A slow, predictable swing that only nudges, never strikes hard; safe to time past.

slots.meaning: Ending: rushing the pendulum crossing instead of timing it.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal, non-toxic, unarmed; a gentle bump back to the safe side, nothing more. Must read as safe.

slots.meaning: The centerpiece mechanism under the rotunda's dome.
slots.guidance: A safe, turnable clockwork fixture.

slots.meaning: The carved base under the globe hiding a sliding-tile puzzle.
slots.guidance: A safe, puzzle-bearing pedestal.

slots.meaning: The night guard's room off the rotunda.
slots.guidance: A safe office-like room.

slots.meaning: The hidden stair the globe/panel/records paths all open onto, leading to the final vault.
slots.guidance: A safe, ordinary descending stair.

slots.meaning: Ending: forcing the globe's rings after guessing wrong instead of decoding properly.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal, non-toxic, unarmed; the rings just lock up, the hero is unhurt, only the route is lost for the night. Must read as safe.

slots.meaning: The founder's hidden private workshop, found via the pedestal's side door.
slots.guidance: A safe, wonder-filled sealed room.

slots.meaning: Ending: discovering and reporting the founder's hidden workshop (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; a shareable historical discovery, nothing taken or harmed.

slots.meaning: The record the guard keeps, readable in the records room.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable logbook.

slots.meaning: The safe way to call for a lift home from the records room.
slots.guidance: A safe, working desk phone.

slots.meaning: Ending: calling home from the records room instead of continuing (neutral).
slots.guidance: Neutral but warm; safe and content, not a failure, just a different good stopping point.

slots.meaning: The founder's secret final room, holding the collection.
slots.guidance: A safe, wonder-filled hidden room.

slots.meaning: The founder's explanatory letter, found in the vault.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable letter.

slots.meaning: The second vault item, marking a further hidden wing.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable old map.

slots.meaning: The full inventory the letter's final clue reveals.
slots.guidance: A safe, readable ledger of the whole collection.

slots.meaning: Ending: following the letter's clue to the full catalogue (the main triumphant success).
slots.guidance: Positive; the whole mystery resolved and given back to the town.

slots.meaning: Ending: keeping the letter safe and leaving the rest untouched (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; a calm, honour-bound outcome.

slots.meaning: The hidden wing the treasure map marks.
slots.guidance: A safe, sealed-off part of the museum.

slots.meaning: What is found inside the walled-up wing.
slots.guidance: A safe gallery of recovered artwork.

slots.meaning: Ending: following the map to the hidden gallery (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; a proud, shareable discovery.

slots.meaning: Ending: photographing the map as evidence instead of exploring further (neutral).
slots.guidance: Neutral; a measured, sensible outcome, leaving the decision to adults.

slots.meaning: Ending: losing track of time tracing the map until staff arrive (setback).
slots.guidance: Non-lethal, non-toxic, unarmed; simply runs out of night and leaves to return later. Must read as safe.

slots.meaning: Ending: waking the curator with proof (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; a warm, complete, celebrated outcome.

slots.meaning: Ending: keeping the discovery a secret with close friends (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; a happy, private bond, nothing harmed or hidden forever from safety.

slots.meaning: Ending: leaving an anonymous clue for the museum to find (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; a quiet, generous outcome.

slots.meaning: The takeable item in the vault the hero can consider grabbing.
slots.guidance: A safe, small keepsake object; not a weapon or hazard.

slots.meaning: Ending: taking the owl and getting caught by its pressure-set alarm (setback).
slots.guidance: Non-lethal, non-toxic, unarmed; a stern word and a handback, no real trouble. Must read as safe.

slots.meaning: Ending: setting the owl back and leaving with only notes (neutral).
slots.guidance: Neutral; a thoughtful, clean-conscience outcome.
